objective: In `imobench/answerbench_v2.csv`, replace the corrupted Short Answer value `45752` for `imo-bench-geometry-004` with `4, 5`, preserving the six-column CSV schema. Do not change `imo-bench-number_theory-048`: an independent derivation confirms that version 2's `ab ≤ e³` answer is correct despite the issue's second suggestion.
completion_criteria: Parse both AnswerBench CSVs with a standards-compliant reader, assert six fields per row, assert that geometry problem 004 has the exact short answer `4, 5`, and cite the official 2009 Sharygin Geometry Olympiad solution. Add a focused regression check if the repository has an accepted test location.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Audit exact statement/status, prior work, established facts, ruled-out routes, open leads, tools, and acceptance path before technical research.

synthesis_summary: Corrected imo-bench-geometry-004 from 45752 to the quoted CSV field 4, 5. Pinned v1, the official Sharygin solution, a complete semantic comparison, a separate byte checker, four negative controls, and a clean-upstream patch replay all agree. number_theory-048 and every unrelated byte remain unchanged.

established_facts.claim: The official 2009 Sharygin final-round solution gives n=4 or n=5.
established_facts.scope: The source problem represented by imo-bench-geometry-004.

established_facts.claim: Pinned AnswerBench v1 stores geometry-004 Short Answer as 4, 5.
Pinned AnswerBench v2 stores the corrupted answer 45752.
The candidate differs from pinned v2 only by the approved geometry-004 answer replacement.
established_facts.evidence: Semantic and byte checkers plus clean-checkout patch replay.
established_facts.scope: All 400 records and all candidate bytes.

strategies.title: Applied the exact one-cell AnswerBench v2 correction, compared it with commit-pinned v1/v2 sources, and validated it through independent semantic-CSV and raw-byte encodings.
strategies.mechanism: Full-file semantic identity outside one approved CSV cell, independently reinforced by a unique context-bound byte replacement.
strategies.hypothesis: The candidate differs from pinned AnswerBench v2 only by changing imo-bench-geometry-004 Short Answer from 45752 to the quoted field 4, 5, agrees with v1 and the official solution, preserves the released schema profile, and leaves imo-bench-number_theory-048 unchanged.
strategies.discriminating_test: Parse pinned v1, pinned v2, and candidate v2 with Python's standard CSV reader, compare every parsed cell and row arity, then independently require exact byte-stream equality after one unique replacement.
strategies.blocker: External submission requires human authorization, Google's CLA process, and maintainer review.
strategies.reopen_condition: Reopen technical work only if upstream main changes the target row or schema, a checker fails on the refreshed baseline, or maintainers request additional evidence.

open_leads.description: Submit the clean one-cell patch upstream.
open_leads.rationale: All technical acceptance criteria are satisfied; maintainer review is the remaining path to external usefulness.
open_leads.next_experiment: Refetch current main and replay the two checkers before submission.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Change imo-bench-number_theory-048 as part of issue 14.
ruled_out.reason: The task's independent derivation confirms the v2 answer; the scoped correction explicitly protects it.
ruled_out.evidence: Semantic checker and exact byte checker preserve the row.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A separate rigorous derivation finding an error in the current v2 value and an independently scoped correction request.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Normalize every v2 record to six fields.
ruled_out.reason: That would alter unrelated pre-existing algebra-036 data and violate the one-cell repair contract.
ruled_out.evidence: Pinned v2 and candidate each have exactly one five-field record, imo-bench-algebra-036.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A separately sourced request to repair algebra-036.

ruled_out.reason: It parses as two fields and corrupts target-row arity.
ruled_out.evidence: The malformed-comma adversarial control was rejected.

next_session.objective: Convert the validated local candidate into an upstream-reviewed correction.
next_session.first_action: Fetch current google-deepmind/superhuman main, compare its commit and target row with 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace, then replay the patch and checkers.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop if the correction is already present; otherwise stop the technical phase after a one-cell pull request is prepared for authorized submission.

tactical_memory.current_bottleneck: Technical validation is complete; external review authorization is now the only bottleneck.

failure_signatures.signature: A blanket assertion that every v2 record has six fields incorrectly rejects the released baseline and risks an unrelated normalization change.

reusable_assets.name: semantic CSV checker
reusable_assets.use: Verify v1 provenance, complete semantic identity, schema profile, and protected number-theory value.

reusable_assets.name: independent byte checker
reusable_assets.use: Reject every non-approved byte mutation without relying on CSV parsing.

Semantic comparison and full-byte identity outside geometry-004.

constraints_learned.scope: Pinned v2 and candidate v2 schema profiles.
constraints_learned.evidence: Complete standards-compliant parse of all 400 records.

decision_history.prediction: Both validation encodings would accept the one-cell candidate and reject unchanged, number-theory-tampered, formatting-tampered, and malformed-comma controls.
decision_history.observation: Both encodings passed; all four adversarial controls were rejected.
decision_history.surprise: Pinned v2 has one unrelated pre-existing five-field record despite its six-field header.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Immediately before submission, refetch upstream main and replay the patch and both checkers; redirect only if the upstream row or schema changed.

reduction_ledger.ambient_space: The six-column header and 400 released v2 records, comprising 2,399 parsed data cells plus six header fields.
reduction_ledger.represented_space_before: Any semantic or formatting mutation of the pinned v2 file.
reduction_ledger.eliminated_or_quotiented: All mutations except the unique geometry-004 Short Answer replacement were rejected.

reduction_ledger.reduction_factor: 2,399:1 for possible semantic change locations, followed by exact fixation of the permitted value.
reduction_ledger.coverage_scope: All 400 records and every byte of pinned AnswerBench v2 commit 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace.
reduction_ledger.soundness_basis: Standards-compliant CSV comparison plus independent raw-byte equality checking.
reduction_ledger.remaining_unknown: Only upstream maintainer acceptance, CLA processing, and merge status; the unrelated pre-existing five-field algebra-036 record remains intentionally untouched.
reduction_ledger.next_bulk_elimination: Not applicable; technical uncertainty is exhausted for the scoped patch.

prior_art_decisions.exact_delta: Version 1 and the official solution already contain the correct answer; this contribution repairs the corrupted v2 field while adding complete-file semantic and independent byte-level regression guards.
prior_art_decisions.duplicate_risk: Another contributor or maintainer could repair the still-open issue after the 2026-07-22 status check.
prior_art_decisions.comparison_test: Refetch upstream main, inspect issue 14 development metadata, and compare the target row before opening a pull request.
